rule_metadata.author: RedCanary, Sigma formatting by Micah Babinski
rule_metadata.title: Suspicious Commands - WMI

rules.rule_text:
title: Suspicious Commands - WMI
id: 5a6a9a1b-97d0-43d8-9dd7-3cdef056c3c3
status: experimental
description: "Looking for suspicious command-line parameters is another solid indicator\
  \ of malice. Certain \nred team and post-exploitation frameworks will spawn unique\
  \ and unsigned binaries or commands \nremotely using the well known process call\
  \ create command, and we\u2019ve got a couple different \ndetection methods that\
  \ have alerted us to related activity over the years. Potentially \nsuspicious WMI\
  \ command switches include create, node:, process, and call. Of course, the \nmaliciousness\
  \ of these commands are context-specific, and therefore, the following may \nrequire\
  \ tuning or generate high volumes of false positives. Part of the RedCanary 2024\
  \ Threat \nDetection Report.\n"
references:
- https://redcanary.com/threat-detection-report/techniques/windows-management-instrumentation/
author: RedCanary, Sigma formatting by Micah Babinski
date: 2024/03/21
tags:
- attack.execution
- attack.t1047
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: \wmic.exe
    CommandLine|contains:
    - create
    - 'node:'
    - process
    - call
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
- Unknown
level: low
